headline: Homebuilders and Healthcare Lead as Semiconductors Drag on a Geopolitically Charged Thursday
body_html:
Midday trade on Thursday, July 16 is broadly constructive, with 295 of 456 names in our universe trading higher against 159 decliners — a solid if unspectacular advance-decline ratio that underscores a market navigating real geopolitical risk rather than ignoring it. Homebuilders pace all sectors with a gain of roughly 1.8%, followed closely by Healthcare at 1.6% and Consumer at 1.5%, suggesting defensive and rate-sensitive names are absorbing capital as investors seek shelter from Middle East uncertainty. Software and Industrials are modestly positive, while Materials and Energy barely register gains.

The session's single sharpest outlier is DexCom, up 7.0% intraday and the clear standout mover of the day — a move that has helped lift Healthcare broadly. Logistics names are running hot as well: JB Hunt has surged nearly 6% and ArcBest is up better than 5%, hinting that freight markets may be pricing in shipping-route disruption following reports of reduced vessel traffic through the Strait of Hormuz. Cintas and Ingersoll Rand round out the top movers, keeping Business Services and Industrials-FlowControl in focus.

The glaring exception is Semiconductors, the session's worst sector by a wide margin at –3.6%. MaxLinear, Sandisk, and Vicor Corporation have each shed more than 7%, and Corning — off 7.5% — and Seagate Technology add to the damage. Into the close, watch whether the chip selloff broadens or stabilizes, and monitor any fresh geopolitical developments out of the Gulf that could shift energy and shipping names sharply in either direction.

briefs.headline: UnitedHealth Beats Estimates, Raises Full-Year Profit Guidance
briefs.body: UnitedHealth delivered quarterly results well ahead of analyst expectations Thursday, with cost discipline driving the outperformance. Management responded by lifting its full-year earnings outlook, providing a credibility boost that has helped anchor the broader Healthcare sector's 1.6% midday advance.

briefs.headline: Hormuz Traffic Thins as US Strikes Target Iranian Port City
briefs.body: The US military confirmed it conducted fresh strikes on Iran, including the port hub of Bandar Abbas, a development that has prompted fewer commercial vessels to transit the Strait of Hormuz. The disruption is drawing attention to global energy and freight supply chains, likely contributing to strength in domestic logistics names.

briefs.headline: Iran Said to Order Houthi Allies to Shut Red Sea Corridor if US Hits Power Grid
briefs.body: Tehran has reportedly instructed Houthi forces in Yemen to close the Red Sea shipping lane should American forces strike Iranian power infrastructure, according to sourced reporting. The contingency threat amplifies existing concerns about global trade routes and adds a second potential chokepoint beyond Hormuz.

briefs.headline: Oil Drifts Lower Despite Escalating Gulf Tensions
briefs.body: Crude prices eased in midday trade even as US-Iran confrontation intensified, as traders appeared to weigh the release of an American detainee as a potential off-ramp for the conflict. The subdued oil reaction has kept the Energy sector nearly flat on the session, gaining just 0.05%.

briefs.headline: Semiconductor Complex Hammered; Sector Drops More Than 3.5%
briefs.body: Technology names tied to chips and power electronics are absorbing the session's heaviest selling pressure, with the Semiconductors sector off 3.6% at midday. MaxLinear, Sandisk, Vicor, and Seagate Technology have each lost more than 6%, pointing to a combination of sector rotation and possible inventory or demand concerns.

briefs.headline: Prediction Markets See Gasoline Crossing Four Dollars by Month-End
briefs.body: Traders on event-contract platform Kalshi have pushed implied odds higher for retail gasoline prices breaching the four-dollar-per-gallon threshold before July closes, according to market data. The wager reflects growing unease that Gulf shipping disruptions could ripple into domestic fuel costs within weeks.
